entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the crop being studied for phenotypic diversity.

entities.name: CORE COLLECTION
entities.description: A set of 1704 groundnut accessions used for evaluation.

entities.name: SUBSPC. FASTIGIATA
entities.description: A subspecies of groundnut comprising varieties fastigiata, vulgaris, aequatoriana, and peruviana.

entities.name: SUBSPC. HYPOGAEA
entities.description: A subspecies of groundnut comprising varieties hypogaea and hirsuta.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variation in observable traits among groundnut accessions.

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL DESCRIPTORS
entities.description: 16 traits used to assess the physical characteristics of the core collection.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: 32 traits used to assess the agricultural performance of the core collection.

entities.name: LEAFLET SURFACE
entities.description: A morphological descriptor used to compare groundnut subspecies.

entities.name: OIL CONTENT
entities.description: An agronomic characteristic used to compare groundnut subspecies.

entities.name: POD LENGTH
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: POD WIDTH
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: SEED LENGTH
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: SEED WIDTH
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the hypogaea group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the fastigiata group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: LEAFLET LENGTH
entities.description: A morphological trait where the fastigiata group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: LEAFLET WIDTH
entities.description: A morphological trait where the fastigiata group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: SHELLING PERCENTAGE
entities.description: An agronomic trait where the fastigiata group showed significantly greater mean.

entities.name: DAYS TO 50% FLOWERING
entities.description: An agronomic trait that explained more than 50% variation in the postrainy season.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COORDINATE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A multivariate analysis method used to identify important morphological descriptors.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A multivariate analysis method used to identify important agronomic traits.

entities.name: MULTIVARIATE POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: The variation across multiple morphological and agronomic traits.

entities.name: LEAFLET SHAPE
entities.description: A morphological descriptor with low importance in explaining variation.

entities.name: COLOUR OF STANDARD PETAL MARKINGS
entities.description: A morphological descriptor with low importance in explaining variation.

entities.name: SEED COLOUR PATTERN
entities.description: A morphological descriptor with low importance in explaining variation.

entities.name: PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: An agronomic trait with low importance in explaining variation.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC DIVERSITY INDEX
entities.description: A measure of diversity found to be similar in both subspecies groups.

entities.name: SHANNON-WEAVER DIVERSITY INDEX
entities.description: A method used to measure diversity among traits between the two groups.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The 1999 growing season used for trait evaluation.

entities.name: POSTRAINY SEASON
entities.description: The 1999/2000 growing season used for trait evaluation.

relationships.description: influenced the diversity within a group
influenced the diversity within a group
